top.title: docs(rules): CRITICAL RULESにRule 13・14・15・16を追加（16項目に更新）
top.body:
## 概要
CLAUDE.md の CRITICAL RULES に Rule 14〜16 を追加し、13項目から16項目へ拡充した。また旧 Rule 11（reflexion再読指示）を削除し、workflow Step 4 に reflexion チェックを直接統合することで間接参照チェーンを解消した。

## 変更内容
- **Rule 14**: 複数 Worktree 環境での WORKTREE_ROOT 境界チェックを強制（意図しない他 Worktree への誤編集防止）。PR#213レビュー対応: git init 危険提案除去・2+エントリ確認失敗時の STOP 明示化
- **Rule 15**: セッション開始時の `~/.claude/tasks/lessons.md` 自動レビューと、明示的な訂正・ネガティブフィードバック受領後の即時追記を義務化。Edit tool のみ使用（Write tool は上書き禁止）。グローバルファイルとしてクロスプロジェクトで蓄積し、20件超でクリーンアップ。同一 Root Cause の訂正が 2 件以上出現した場合はユーザーに構造的ルール改善を報告
- **Rule 16**: 自律バグ修正の許可スコープを明確化。✅ 自律修正OK: `tests/**/test_*.py`・`*.py` ロジックエラー（下記セ

top.title: feat(channel/bb): group mention gating — require_mention_in_groups
top.body:
## Summary

- Base branch target: `main`
- Problem: In group iMessage chats the bot responds to every message, making it intrusive when multiple people are conversing. No way to require an explicit @mention before the agent engages.
- Why it matters: Mention gating is a standard group-chat UX pattern required for feature parity with OpenClaw's BlueBubbles extension and for polite group deployments.
- What changed: Three new config keys on `BlueBubblesConfig`: `require_mention_in_groups: bool` (global flag, default `false`), `mention_keyword: Option<String>` (keyword to match, defaults to first

top.title: feat(tools/bb): reply, edit, and unsend message actions
top.body:
## Summary

- Base branch target: `main`
- Problem: The LLM agent cannot reply to a specific iMessage thread, edit a sent message, or retract a sent message — limiting conversational accuracy.
- Why it matters: Thread-aware reply, edit, and unsend are standard iMessage features required for full agent parity with OpenClaw's BlueBubbles extension.
- What changed: New `bluebubbles_message` tool with a single `action` dispatch (`reply`, `edit`, `unsend`). Uses BB private-API endpoints: reply via `selectedMessageGuid`, edit via `/message/{guid}/edit`, unsend via `/message/{guid}/unsend`. Registere

top.title: [PPL Autocomplete] Adopt backend grammar bundle as runtime source of truth with safe fallback
top.body:
### Description
This PR makes PPL autocomplete runtime-grammar first by consuming the backend grammar bundle API, while keeping compiled autocomplete as the unchanged fallback.

#### Key Changes
- Switched runtime autocomplete to use backend grammar bundle metadata (tokenDictionary, ignoredTokens, rulesToVisit) via LexerInterpreter/ParserInterpreter + C3.
- Fixed runtime correctness issues:
  - C3 cache isolation per grammarHash (prevents ghost token IDs).
  - rerun logic when preferred rules hide token suggestions.
  - filter invalid token candidates (EOF / no vocab mapping).
  - pip

top.title: feat(cli): add configurable backend image fallback tags via CLI options
top.body:
## Summary

Add three new CLI options to configure backend image fallback patterns:
- --backend-images-release-tag: Configure the release tag (default: 'latest')
- --backend-images-branch-tag: Configure the branch tag (default: 'master')
- --backend-dev-suffix: Configure the development suffix (default: 'development')

These options propagate to InstallBackend via environment variables, allowing users to customize fallback URI resolution for backend gallery installations.

This is a follow-up to PR #8663 which added the fallback logic but hardcoded the tag values.

top.title: Add cloud manager infrastructure for Azure and CoreWeave
top.body:
## Description
- Introduces Cloud Manager infrastructure for managing cloud-based Kubernetes clusters (Azure AKS and CoreWeave) as separate controller binaries with dedicated CRDs (`AzureKubernetesEngine`, `CoreWeaveKubernetesEngine`)
- Adds a generic Helm chart rendering action (`pkg/controller/actions/render/helm/`) and cloud manager reconciliation framework (`pkg/controller/cloudmanager/`) with hooks support for provider-specific customization
- Includes build tooling (Makefile targets, Dockerfile updates, manifest generation), Kubernetes deployment manifests, and Helm chart dependency m

top.title: Preprocessing improvements
top.body:
Purpose
Improves preprocessing speed in BasePredictor


We accomplish these changes by allowing gpu accelerated tensor ops and using non blocking device transfer. We also add explicit branching between list[np.ndarray] case and Tensor case for better readability. 

I also think there may be a possible bug on line 185 (of the diff) of ultralytics/engine/predictor.py? so the original comment states that they can expect uint8 inputs for Tensor, but doesn't actually shift from 0-255 to 0-1.0 which would cause big issues for the model. I am wondering if intended behavior Tensor inputs should

top.title: add CUDA SWA block-table sanitizer
top.body:
<!-- .github/pull_request_template.md -->

## 📌 Description
Reported by VLLM: SWA + prefix cache enabled + hybrid KV cache manager + TRTLLM attention backend leads to corrupted generations due to incorrectly reading kv cache null blocks. This MR adds sanitization for SWA TRTLLM paged-KV block tables.

The reproducer script provided now passes with negligible performance impact.

## 🔍 Related Issues

https://nvbugspro.nvidia.com/bug/5922676

## 🚀 Pull Request Checklist

Thank you for contributing to FlashInfer! Before we review your pull request, please make sure the following item

top.title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix SQL injection in RTBF Audit queries
top.body:
🚨 **Severity**: CRITICAL
💡 **Vulnerability**: `RTBFAuditService` dynamically constructed SQL count and sample queries by concatenating raw, user-provided `table` and `field` identifiers into the query string without validation or escaping.
🎯 **Impact**: A malicious actor could inject arbitrary SQL commands (e.g., `; DROP TABLE users; --`) by supplying crafted payloads in the `table` or `field` properties of the `RTBFTarget` structure, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or complete data loss.
🔧 **Fix**: Introduced a `validateIdentifier` method using a strict regex (`/^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+

top.title: MX-145: MCP Server Implementation for Mifos X (Python implementation)
top.body:
PR Description: Context-Aware Banking AI Copilot
This PR introduces a decoupled, API-first architecture for a Context-Aware Banking AI Copilot. It establishes a deterministic, hallucination-free integration tier between the LLM and Apache Fineract, abstracting and validating all banking operations before any AI orchestration executes.

Key Architecture & Features
1️⃣ Stateless MCP Adapter
Authenticates against Fineract while natively handling local SSL constraints.
Transforms legacy Fineract JSON responses into clean, structured Python dictionaries.
Slims down verbose Fineract responses

top.title: feat: add Avian as a named LLM provider
top.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class support for [Avian](https://avian.io) as a named LLM provider, following the same pattern as the Tinfoil provider added in #62.

Avian is an OpenAI-compatible inference service providing access to DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, and MiniMax models. This PR adds it as a dedicated backend (`LLM_BACKEND=avian`) rather than requiring users to configure it through the generic `openai_compatible` path.

### Changes

- **`src/config/llm.rs`** — `LlmBackend::Avian` enum variant, `AvianConfig` struct, `AVIAN_API_KEY` / `AVIAN_MODEL` env var resolution (default model: `deepseek/deepsee

top.title: fileserver: migrate pingcap/tidb release-8.5 artifact downloads to OCI
top.body:
## Summary
- split out `pipelines/pingcap/tidb/release-8.5/**` changes from #4274 into a standalone PR
- migrate `release-8.5` pipelines away from `fileserver.pingcap.net` to OCI artifact pulls
- keep implementation pattern aligned with migrated `latest` jobs (`OCI_TAG_*` + `download_pingcap_oci_artifact.sh` via `utils` container)

## Scope
- 30 files under `pipelines/pingcap/tidb/release-8.5/`

## Validation
- Jenkinsfile validation succeeded for all changed Groovy files via `.ci/verify-jenkins-pipeline-file.sh`

Ref #4274
Fixes #4292

top.title: feat(api): add logging, rate limits, uploads, SSE realtime, and Stripe billing integrations
top.body:
### Motivation
- Improve observability and request diagnostics by adding structured HTTP logging and request ids via `pino`/`pino-http`.
- Protect critical endpoints with layered rate limiting for auth, tracking, and general traffic using `express-rate-limit`.
- Provide developer-friendly document uploads and serve uploaded files locally, and add a simple Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream for realtime shipment updates.
- Integrate Stripe PaymentIntent creation and webhook handling to record payments and update Prisma-backed invoices/transactions.

### Description
- Updated `apps/api/package.json
